LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 7, 8, 9, 11, AND 12. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BASED ON THE CALIFORNIA STATE LAW DUTY TO WARN. DEFENDANT CANNOT ESTABLISH, BASED ON THE COMPLAINT, THAT THE DUTY TO WARN CONFLICTS WITH APPLICABLE GOVERNMENT SPECIFICATIONS. AS A CONSEQUENCE, PLAINTIFF'S WAIVER OF FEDERAL LAW CLAIMS IS NOT A WAIVER OF STATE LAW CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY PLEADS STATE LAW CLAIMS BASED ON EXPOSURE AND FAILURE TO WARN. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Jan-13-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Allison J. Fairchild, Esq. appeared for Petitioner Susan Petersen, Guardian ad litem for the minor, Nathaniel Fisher. Neither petitioner not minor were present at today's hearing.
Order granting waiver of appearance of guardian ad litem at hearing was filed today, January 13, 2009.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Settlement Amount - $20,000.00.
Attorney's Fees - $7,330.00.
Costs - $12,670.00.

A net balance of $0.00 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America - Virginia Hills Branch, located at 626 Main Street, Martinez, CA 94553. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years. The Court approves the compromise and release of any and all future settlements negotiated in this matter pursuant to the terms herein, namely Nathaniel Fisher to receive 10% of net settlement proceeds.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor, and to Deposit Money Into Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.
